summary: CR expires Jan 30 2026, 43-day shutdown just ended Nov 2025, Democrats threaten another shutdown
content: Key findings: The continuing resolution expires January 30, 2026 - just 1 day before the Feb 1 resolution deadline. A 43-day shutdown ended Nov 12, 2025. Democrats have explicitly threatened another shutdown. Need 60 Senate votes for bipartisan passage. By Jan 16 (the CP check date), Congress will still be 14 days from deadline, making resolution very likely still uncertain. The 33.30% threshold is low enough that CP should exceed it in most scenarios where negotiations are still ongoing.
